_meta.description: QRDI/QNRF funded research projects related to transportation/infrastructure in Qatar. Collected 2026-05-12 via web search.
_meta.limitations: QGrants and QRDI Connect project portal both require authenticated access for full project listings. Many older NPRP grant numbers surface only via the acknowledgements section of resulting papers. Funding amounts are rarely disclosed publicly. This list is therefore non-exhaustive — a substantial fraction of NPRP cycles 1-13 transport projects are not surfaced through Google. Treat as a representative sample, not a complete census.

funding_landscape.QNRF_NPRP_history: Over first 6 years, QNRF NPRP funded 700+ projects totaling >USD 600M across all topics (RAND 2008+).
funding_landscape.QRDI_restructure: Council restructured ~2022 into three pillars: Academic Research Program, National Research Program (NRP), Research Talent Program.
NRP_calls_2024_2025: Climate Change and Environment Call (2nd cycle, Jan 2025)
Qatar Logistics and Transportation Call (QLTC, Jul 2025)
funding_landscape.key_signal_2025: July 2025: QRDI Council announced 30 awards across 6 Rapid Research Calls — QLTC is the explicit transportation/logistics vertical, partnered with Qatar Airways, focused on Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF). Awarded to HBKU, QU, UDST.

projects.project_title: Modeling and Control of Information-Driven Smart Transportation Systems
Analysis of road traffic crashes in the State of Qatar (NPRP 9-360-2-150)
projects.note: Grant cited in 2019 Tandfonline crash analysis paper

projects.project_title: CopITS - Cooperative ITS for V2V/V2X communications (NPRP 3)
Simulation and Modeling of Small/Medium-Sized Transportation and Communication Networks (NPRP 5-1272-1-214)
Rapid urban development and national master planning in Arab Gulf countries - Qatar case study (NPRP 5-074-5-015)
Transit-Oriented Development in Middle Eastern Cities: Souq Waqif Urban Renewal Framework
Qatar Logistics and Transportation Call (QLTC) - awards bundle (7 institutions, 30 total awards across 6 calls)
projects.institution: Hamad Bin Khalifa University, Qatar University, University of Doha for Science and Technology
projects.note: Partnered with Qatar Airways. Focus on SAF — aviation-emphasized, NOT urban roads/congestion.

projects.funding_program: QRDI NRP - Scientific Research Programs Office

projects.project_title: $130M+ to Qatar research projects across multiple cycles
projects.note: Doha News reporting; transportation share unspecified but historically a minority of NPRP portfolio.

observations.transportation_share: Transportation is a recurring NPRP theme but not dominant. Qatar's research funding skews toward: energy, health, ICT, materials. The 2025 QLTC explicitly signals transportation/logistics priority — but with strong SAF/aviation/maritime emphasis, less so urban congestion or road-network supply-demand analysis.
observations.named_PIs_clustered: Wael Alhajyaseen (QTTSC, QU) appears to be the dominant Qatar-based transportation researcher by publication count. Eyad Masad (TAMU-Q) is the senior pavement/transportation engineer. Hamid Menouar (QMIC) handles V2X/ITS infrastructure.
data_sources_used_in_prior_grants: Qatar Ministry of Interior crash data (Alhajyaseen)
observations.gap_signal: Very little surfaced funded work using PSA/Comtrade economic data, satellite imagery, big mobility data (TomTom/HERE/Mapbox), or longitudinal OSM history. Most funded work is microscale (crashes, simulators, surveys at specific roundabouts/sites).
